meta.pivotLine: From the family game room to the curated social home.
meta.positioning: Billiard Factory has spent fifty years as one of the nation's largest billiard and game-room furniture retailers. Our owned brands are engineered, designed, and finished by our factories, we carry the best brands in the category, and we sell through our own showrooms: a family-owned house now curating the modern social home.

brandFoundations.purpose: Enabling families and friends to share priceless time together, one game room at a time.
brandFoundations.mission: Since 1975, our mission has been simple: enable families and friends to share priceless time together, one game room at a time. We aren't in the furniture business; we're in the business of the time that turns out to be priceless.
brandFoundations.promise: We work hard to earn your business: the highest-quality game rooms and the kind of top-notch service that comes from a family-owned house that's done this for fifty years.

values.name: Family Focus
values.body: We respect the family unit as the cornerstone of social and moral development, and we champion family-based recreation for the stronger bonds it builds between people.

values.name: Customer Commitment
values.body: Our pledge: we work hard to earn your business, striving to give every customer the highest-quality products and top-notch service.

values.name: Community Support
values.body: When our communities call on us, we answer, giving time, resources, and support, and intertwining compassion with integrity in every endeavor.

values.name: Employee Care
values.body: Our people are the heartbeat of our success. We want every workday to end in the satisfaction of effort well spent.

persona.name: The Host
persona.body: Thirty-five to sixty, building a home they intend to keep and fill with people. They see a game room as where the family actually spends time: birthdays, snow days, the long Friday night. They want quality that lasts and a room the whole family is drawn to, at a price that reads as value, not luxury tax.
speaksTo: Families furnishing a great room or finished basement
Designers and builders specifying for clients

tokens.proportionLaw: Cream & Mist 60 · Heritage Green + photography 30 · Walnut + Orange 10. Orange never exceeds 5%. It earns attention by being rare.

proportion.label: Surface
proportion.note: Cream & Mist, paper, breathing room, the gallery wall.

proportion.label: Brand
proportion.note: Heritage Green & photography, the colour that carries the story.

proportion.label: Warmth
proportion.note: Walnut, wood tones pulled from the product itself.

proportion.label: Signal
proportion.note: Orange, calls to action only. Rare on purpose.

sans.note: The brand face. Heavy (85) for headlines at -30 tracking; Light (45) for subheads.

serif.note: Warm reading serif for story and long-form.

mono.note: The spec-sheet voice, a monospaced companion drawn from Helvetica's skeleton. Carries hex values, measurements, economics figures, kickers, and status flags. One weight, used sparingly.

scale.name: Subhead
scale.specimen: We make it. We curate it. We sell it.

scale.name: Editorial
scale.specimen: The room where a family gathers.

scale.name: Body
scale.specimen: Solid-hardwood game-room furniture, finished by hand and engineered to be inherited.

logo.clearSpace: Keep clear space equal to the height of the cube mark on all four sides. Nothing (type, photo edge, or another logo) enters that zone.
logo.minSize: Full lockup: 32px tall on screen, 0.5in in print. Simple mark: 20px / 0.25in. Below that, use the simple mark.

variations.name: Full lockup · black
variations.use: Default. Light backgrounds: Cream, Mist, white.

variations.name: Simple mark · black
variations.use: Tight spaces, favicons, nav on light.

variations.name: Simple mark · on white
variations.use: Plain mark on pure white: packaging, documents, co-branding.

variations.name: Simple mark · on black
variations.use: Plain mark reversed on black: apparel, dark packaging.

variations.name: Simple mark · on Heritage Green
variations.use: Plain mark on the brand green: signage, vehicle, felt.

variations.name: Simple mark · on Signal Orange
variations.use: Plain mark reversed on the accent: promo tags, event pieces.

misuse: Don't stretch, condense, or skew the lockup, scale proportionally only.
Don't recolour the logo outside the approved black/white set.
Don't add drop shadows, outlines, glows, or bevels.
Don't place the black logo on a dark or busy photo, switch to white.
Don't rotate the mark or rebuild it from other type.
Don't crowd the clear-space zone with text or graphics.

voice.summary: Warm, confident, family-rooted, never stuffy. We sell the room a family gathers in, not a unit on a warehouse floor.

principles.name: Family-rooted, not corporate
principles.body: Speak like a maker who knows the customer's name. Plain, warm, specific. Never sound like a press release.

principles.name: Confident, never boastful
principles.body: Fifty years and our own brands let us state facts plainly. Show the proof, skip the hype.

principles.name: Concrete over clever
principles.body: Name the slate, the hardwood, the tolerance. Specifics earn trust; adjectives don't.

principles.name: Earn the accent
principles.body: One exclamation point, ever. Orange and urgency are rare on purpose. Restraint reads as premium.

mechanics: Maximum one exclamation point per piece.
Spell one through nine; use figures for 10 and up, and for all prices.
If a claim isn't supported, ask or omit it.

beforeAfter.before: Look no further, our robust, game-changing tables boast premium quality!
beforeAfter.after: One-inch diamond-honed slate, solid hardwood, finished by hand. Built to be re-leveled, restored, and inherited.

beforeAfter.before: Nestled in today's fast-paced world, we elevate your man cave experience.
beforeAfter.after: Fifty years of building the room a family gathers in. Made, curated, and sold under one roof.

beforeAfter.before: Don't miss our seamless clearance event on game room units!
beforeAfter.after: Our spring great-room event is on. Factory-direct pricing on the tables that anchor the room.

imagery.direction: Warm, lived-in, architectural. The room reads as a place a family actually gathers, natural light, real materials, the product framed as furniture first. Wood grain and felt are the textures; green and walnut are the tones.
do: Real rooms and real light, golden, directional, late-afternoon.
Lifestyle context: the table in a designed great room, not isolated on white.
Tight detail shots of slate, hardwood, felt, and stitching that prove the craft.
Warm wood and Heritage Green tones; let walnut carry the warmth.
dont: No cold studio strobe or white seamless catalog cutouts.
No oversaturated showroom lighting or clinical fluorescents.
No man-cave neon, plastic finishes, or CGI sheen.
No warped perspective, cartoon, or stock-photo staging.
imagery.negativePrompt: --no cold studio strobe, white seamless background, clinical catalog cutout, oversaturated showroom lighting, man-cave neon, plastic finish, warped perspective, cartoon, CGI sheen

history.origin: A single store on Telephone Road in Houston, Texas, four employees and a workshop.
history.today: One of the nation's largest billiard and game-room furniture retailers: 11 showrooms, multiple distribution centers, still family-owned.

timeline.title: One store, four people
timeline.body: Larry Stick opens the first Billiard Factory on Telephone Road in Houston, a single showroom built on the belief that a game room is where a family gathers.

timeline.title: Out of Houston
timeline.body: Rapid growth adds stores across the Houston suburbs, then the first locations beyond Texas, Scottsdale, Arizona and San Antonio.

timeline.title: Into Dallas–Fort Worth
timeline.body: The acquisition of seven Billiards & Barstools stores carries the brand into the DFW metroplex, growth through both building and buying.

timeline.title: The second generation
timeline.body: Stewardship passes to the Stick family's second generation, Tony and Ryan, committed to one of the widest, deepest product lines in the industry.

timeline.title: One owner, one vision
timeline.body: Ryan Stick assumes full ownership and begins uniting the owned brands, distribution, and retail into a single house.

timeline.title: Fifty years
timeline.body: A half-century in, Billiard Factory turns from the family game room toward the curated social home, recreation, elevated to curation.

productRange.intro: Nine categories, one of the deepest assortments in the country, from tournament-grade tables to the furniture that frames the room.

categories.name: Pool Tables
categories.blurb: Masterpiece, dining, tournament, modern, rustic, plus nine signature collections.

categories.name: Shuffleboard
categories.blurb: Solid-hardwood playfields from 9 to 22 feet, indoor and outdoor.

categories.name: Game Tables
categories.blurb: Multi-game sets and convertible dining tops that work for game night and dinner.

categories.name: Cues & Cases
categories.blurb: McDermott, Lucasi, Players and more, plus cases, chalk, and storage.

categories.name: Air Hockey
categories.blurb: Commercial-grade rails and blowers for true competitive play.

categories.name: Foosball
categories.blurb: From the Tornado tournament standard to family-room favorites.

categories.name: Ping Pong
categories.blurb: Indoor and outdoor table tennis, tournament to backyard.

categories.name: Arcade & Pinball
categories.blurb: Pinball machines, arcade cabinets, and golf, the full game room.

categories.name: Furniture
categories.blurb: Bar stools, pub tables, theater seating, and the Himalayan Collection.

verticalIntegration.thesis: Most retailers only sell. Billiard Factory owns its brands and our factories build them, so there is no middleman between the workshop and your room.

layers.label: We Make
layers.body: Owned and family brands engineered, designed, and finished by our factories, protected margins, tournament tolerances, and a standard no reseller can match.

layers.label: We Curate
layers.body: The best manufacturers in the category, carried under one roof and one standard, distribution power that fills the showroom.

layers.label: We Sell
layers.body: Eleven showrooms and a national e-commerce store, and we own the relationship from the factory floor to the family room.

ownedBrands.name: Billiard Factory
copy: The parent brand and the house line, Challenger shuffleboards, air hockey, foosball, and multi-game tables built to Billiard Factory's own spec.
Fifty years of retail taught us exactly what a family room needs. Now we build it ourselves, factory-direct, with no markup in between.

ownedBrands.name: C.L. Bailey
copy: Solid-hardwood game-room furniture on one-inch diamond-honed slate, finished by hand and engineered to be re-leveled, restored, and inherited.
The Skylar, Tunbridge, and Viking collections turn a pool table into furniture first and a game table second.

ownedBrands.name: Level Best
copy: The performance line, tournament-grade cues balanced to competition tolerances and custom-fit covers cut to each table's exact silhouette.
Built in our own factory, so the professional standard carries no luxury tax.

ownedBrands.name: Velocity
copy: The contemporary line, sleek, low, and engineered for modern, minimalist interiors where the table reads as built-in cabinetry, not recreation. These are our competition tables, built for commercial use or the home.
Designed for the loft and the glass-walled great room: a table that holds the sight line instead of interrupting it.

ownedBrands.name: Golden West
copy: The atelier at the top of the house, high-end custom artisanal wood, hand-selected hardwoods and bespoke inlay drawn to a single residence.
The bench where vertical integration becomes visible: a one-of-one object only possible because the factory and the design house share a roof.

partnerBrands.name: Mali Cloth
partnerBrands.note: Worsted tournament cloth, the felt under the game.

partnerBrands.name: Brunswick
partnerBrands.note: The most storied name in billiards, carried under one roof.

partnerBrands.name: Rhino Cues
partnerBrands.note: Specialist cue line rounding out the wall.

moatPillars.label: Fifty years of game-room expertise, a brand story that sells itself.
A real retail footprint plus national e-commerce and distribution centers.
Owned brands built by our factories, factory-direct from the bench to the room.
Protected operating territory for every qualified operator.

demandEngineSteps.title: National Awareness, Handled
demandEngineSteps.body: The parent brand runs national campaigns, PR, and content. Demand arrives at your territory pre-warmed.

demandEngineSteps.title: One-Click Local Deployment
demandEngineSteps.body: Open the dashboard, choose a pre-built campaign, set your radius. Localized creative renders against the live brand tokens.

demandEngineSteps.title: Zero Creative Overhead
demandEngineSteps.body: Every asset is pre-approved and updated centrally. When the brand evolves, your local assets re-skin themselves.

demandEngineSteps.title: Attributed Leads, Routed
demandEngineSteps.body: Qualified leads flow into your CRM with source attribution. You spend your time closing, not making creative.

aiOps.systemPrompt: You are the copywriter for Billiard Factory, America's game room since 1975 and a family-owned house of game-room brands. Voice: warm, confident, family-rooted, never stuffy. We sell the room a family gathers in. Facts: founded 1975 by Larry Stick in Houston; 11 showrooms; family-owned; owned brands (Billiard Factory, C.L. Bailey, Level Best, Velocity, Golden West) plus the best carried brands (Brunswick, Olhausen, Presidential, and more). Mechanics: Oxford comma always; max one exclamation point; spell one–nine, figures for 10+ and all prices. Banned: man cave, game room toys, cheap, clearance, unit. If a claim isn't supported, ask or omit.
aiOps.negativePrompt: --no cold studio strobe, white seamless background, clinical catalog cutout, oversaturated showroom lighting, man-cave neon, plastic finish, warped perspective, cartoon, CGI sheen
